subject: Social and Political Life– II
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the full journey of a shirt from cotton to market
Analyze roles of different participants and profit distribution
Compare prices and fairness in trade
Observe challenges faced by workers and traders
Develop research, analytical, and presentation skills

lesson_plan.title: Journey of a Shirt: In Depth
topics_covered: Stages: cotton farming, ginning, spinning, weaving, tailoring, retail
Key activities and challenges at each stage
student_activities: Create a flowchart of shirt production
List challenges faced at each stage

lesson_plan.title: Roles of People in the Market
topics_covered: Farmers, weavers, factory workers, traders, retailers
Basic income and tasks at each stage
teaching_strategy: Use props or role cards for each participant
student_activities: Assign role cards and describe their work
Discuss who might face difficulties and why

lesson_plan.title: Analyzing Prices and Profits
topics_covered: How prices increase from production to retail
Simple calculation of costs and profits
teaching_strategy: Show examples of price markup using simple numbers
student_activities: Calculate approximate profit at each stage
Discuss which stage earns the most and why

lesson_plan.title: Fairness and Worker Challenges
topics_covered: Difficulties faced by workers and farmers
teaching_strategy: Show real examples or case studies
student_activities: Suggest one improvement or fair practice

lesson_plan.title: Connecting to Real Markets
topics_covered: Comparing textbook journey to real-life experience
teaching_strategy: Ask students about items they buy
Discuss production and pricing in their local market
student_activities: List items bought and their journey to store

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Quiz, and Reflection
topics_covered: Recap of journey, roles, costs, fairness
Relating past learning to everyday shopping
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures and scenario questions
Class discussion connecting chapter to real life
student_activities: Share one new thing learned about production or trade

teaching_aids: Pictures or videos of cotton farming, weaving, tailoring, and markets
Role cards or props for different market participants
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze, discuss, and relate concepts to real-life markets. Use role-play, diagrams, and flowcharts to make learning interactive and reflective.
